author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon XXI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That monks are not to leave their monasteries and go into others.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: A monk or nun ought not to leave the monastery to which he or she is attached, and betake themselves to others.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But if one do this, he ought to be received as a guest.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is not however proper that he be made a member of the monastery, without the consent of his hegumenos.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is not allowed to a monk or a nun to leave her own house and enter another; but if he (or she) enters let (him or her) be received as a guest; but let him (or her) not be admitted at all nor given hospitality contrary to the will of the superior.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: The present canon does not allow a monk or a nun who goes to another house to be received into, nor even to be admitted as a guest, lest by force of necessity he be led astray to worldly things and so remain.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Moreover it does not permit a woman to be admitted and received and reckoned in the number of the sisters without the consent of the superior.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It seems to me that in Aristenus an οὐκ must have crept into the text and that the first sentence should read as now but omitting the “not.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: This makes him agree with Zonaras who says “the man must be received as a guest lest he go to a profane tavern and be forced to associate with those who have never learned how to live decently.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is clear that the “superior” referred to is that of the house whence the monk or nun went forth.
